title: Tremulina tremula
taxonomy: Tremulina tremula is classified as a genus within the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as polypods. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The family Polypodiaceae is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, and Tremulina tremula is a part of the subfamily Pleurodiidae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant. Further research is required to determine the exact relationships within the family and genus.

properties: Tremulina tremula, also known as the Silver Birch, has been used in traditional medicine for its various therapeutic properties. Its bark is rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The plant has also been used to treat fever, coughs, and other respiratory issues. In addition, Tremulina tremula has been used to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of arthritis. Its antimicrobial properties may also help to prevent infections and promote wound healing.
